SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.mechanicaldesign

Class DesignConditions

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.mechanicaldesign.DesignConditions
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public final class DesignConditions extends Object implements Serializable
Lightweight holder for the explicit nameplate design conditions of a piece of process equipment.

Where MechanicalDesign carries the computed mechanical result of a sizing run (wall thickness, weight, sized diameters), this class carries the small set of declared engineering-data-sheet values that a P&ID-driven HAZOP or safety review needs regardless of whether a sizing calculation has been run:

  • Design pressure — the maximum allowable working pressure (MAWP) from the data sheet.
  • Maximum / minimum design temperature — the upper design temperature and the minimum design metal temperature (MDMT) the material remains tough at.
  • Relief set pressure — the set pressure of the protecting pressure safety valve (PSV).
  • Construction material — the nameplate material specification.
  • Corrosion allowance — the corrosion allowance from the data sheet.
  • Failure action — the fail-safe position of a valve actuator on loss of motive power/signal.

Every value is optional. Numeric values default to Double.NaN and isXxxSet() helpers report whether a value has been supplied, so a consumer (for example the DEXPI writer) can export only the populated attributes. The holder is Serializable so it can be stored directly on a process equipment item and survive cloning.

Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL
See Also:

      public DesignConditions()

  • Method Details

    • setDesignPressure

      public DesignConditions setDesignPressure(double bara)
      Sets the design pressure (maximum allowable working pressure, MAWP).
      Parameters:
      bara - design pressure in bara
      Returns:
      this holder for chaining

    • getDesignPressure

      public double getDesignPressure()
      Gets the design pressure (maximum allowable working pressure, MAWP).
      Returns:
      design pressure in bara, or Double.NaN if not set

    • isDesignPressureSet

      public boolean isDesignPressureSet()
      Indicates whether a design pressure has been set.
      Returns:
      true if a design pressure value has been supplied

    • setMaxDesignTemperature

      public DesignConditions setMaxDesignTemperature(double celsius)
      Sets the maximum design temperature.
      Parameters:
      celsius - maximum design temperature in degrees Celsius
      Returns:
      this holder for chaining

    • getMaxDesignTemperature

      public double getMaxDesignTemperature()
      Gets the maximum design temperature.
      Returns:
      maximum design temperature in degrees Celsius, or Double.NaN if not set

    • isMaxDesignTemperatureSet

      public boolean isMaxDesignTemperatureSet()
      Indicates whether a maximum design temperature has been set.
      Returns:
      true if a maximum design temperature value has been supplied

    • setMinDesignTemperature

      public DesignConditions setMinDesignTemperature(double celsius)
      Sets the minimum design metal temperature (MDMT).
      Parameters:
      celsius - minimum design metal temperature in degrees Celsius
      Returns:
      this holder for chaining

    • getMinDesignTemperature

      public double getMinDesignTemperature()
      Gets the minimum design metal temperature (MDMT).
      Returns:
      minimum design metal temperature in degrees Celsius, or Double.NaN if not set

    • isMinDesignTemperatureSet

      public boolean isMinDesignTemperatureSet()
      Indicates whether a minimum design metal temperature has been set.
      Returns:
      true if a minimum design metal temperature value has been supplied

    • setReliefSetPressure

      public DesignConditions setReliefSetPressure(double bara)
      Sets the relief (PSV) set pressure protecting the equipment.
      Parameters:
      bara - relief set pressure in bara
      Returns:
      this holder for chaining

    • getReliefSetPressure

      public double getReliefSetPressure()
      Gets the relief (PSV) set pressure protecting the equipment.
      Returns:
      relief set pressure in bara, or Double.NaN if not set

    • isReliefSetPressureSet

      public boolean isReliefSetPressureSet()
      Indicates whether a relief set pressure has been set.
      Returns:
      true if a relief set pressure value has been supplied

      Sets the corrosion allowance.
      Parameters:
      millimetre - corrosion allowance in mm
      Returns:
      this holder for chaining

    • getCorrosionAllowance

      public double getCorrosionAllowance()
      Gets the corrosion allowance.
      Returns:
      corrosion allowance in mm, or Double.NaN if not set

    • isCorrosionAllowanceSet

      public boolean isCorrosionAllowanceSet()
      Indicates whether a corrosion allowance has been set.
      Returns:
      true if a corrosion allowance value has been supplied

    • setConstructionMaterial

      public DesignConditions setConstructionMaterial(String material)
      Sets the construction material specification.
      Parameters:
      material - the nameplate construction material, for example "Carbon steel" or "Duplex 22Cr"
      Returns:
      this holder for chaining

    • getConstructionMaterial

      public String getConstructionMaterial()
      Gets the construction material specification.
      Returns:
      the construction material, or null if not set

    • isConstructionMaterialSet

      public boolean isConstructionMaterialSet()
      Indicates whether a construction material has been set.
      Returns:
      true if a non-empty construction material has been supplied

    • setFailureAction

      public DesignConditions setFailureAction(DesignConditions.FailureAction action)
      Sets the valve fail-safe action.
      Parameters:
      action - the fail-safe action; null is treated as DesignConditions.FailureAction.NOT_SPECIFIED
      Returns:
      this holder for chaining

    • getFailureAction

      public DesignConditions.FailureAction getFailureAction()
      Gets the valve fail-safe action.
      Returns:
      the fail-safe action, never null

    • isFailureActionSet

      public boolean isFailureActionSet()
      Indicates whether a fail-safe action has been specified.
      Returns:
      true if the fail-safe action is anything other than DesignConditions.FailureAction.NOT_SPECIFIED

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      Indicates whether any design condition value has been supplied.
      Returns:
      true if at least one design condition has been set

    • toJson

      public String toJson()
      Serialise these design conditions to a pretty-printed JSON object.
      Returns:
      JSON representation of the design conditions
